Greek-Entry.English-Entry: to be of a violet colour
τὸ δι' αὐλοῦ παριαμβίζειν τῇ κιθάρᾳ καὶ ᾠδῇ
Iaso, the goddess of healing and health
drink prepared from honey, wine and violets
surgeon who practises by anointing, friction, and the like
doctor's fee, expense of a cure
those who practised medicine in conjunction with astrology
doctor who uses the knife, surgeon, Princeton Exp.Inscr.
Graffītes Grecs du Memnonion d' Abydos
maker of shrouds for the sacred ibis,
musical instrument named after the poet Ibycus
a dance, in which the loins were moved like a pestle,
the part behind the thigh and knee, ham
one who loves at first sight
to be peculiar to an individual
follow one's own counsel, take one's own way
breeding only with one's own kind
written with one's own hand, autograph
of the same length each way
to be invested with a specific character
by oneself, privately, on one's own account
to be sown by one's own labour,
sowing carried out by one's own labour,
land sown by the landlord's own employees,
occupy a congenial position in the zodiac
one who has no professional knowledge, layman
not done by rules of art, unprofessional, amateurish
who had statues erected to them
anything earned by the sweat of one's brow
stone of the colour of a hawk's neck
hold office as commissioner of sacred rites,
as a sacrifice for one's country
to be burnt as a sacrifice
one who takes charge of a temple
founder of a sanctuary, Cat. Cod.Astr.
sacrifices offered during the sacred month,
representative sent by each Amphictyonic state
magistrate who had charge of temples
one who initiates in sacred things
overseer of temples and sacred rites
one who conveys the sacred tribute
title of poem on sacred vestments
who had charge of the sacred vestments
hierophant, one who teaches rites of sacrifice and worship
the parts of a victim which were the priest's perquisites,
make to sit, seat, place, set
to be eager, desire to do
to be set upon, long for
τὸ ἰθὺ πεφυκὸς καὶ ὀρθὸν δένδρον
to be visited with the penalty
τὸ σιδήριον τοῦ ἄξονος τὸ τριβόμενον
curved in one direction, though straight in another
fascinum erectum, the phallos carried in the festivals of Bacchus
one who danced in such dance
to be made complete, brought to perfection
the prayer of a suppliant, supplication
olive-branch which the suppliant held in his hand
one who comes to seek aid
one who comes to seek for purification after homicide
to be wetted, to be wet
a plant growing in moist places, duckweed, Lemna minor
to be ill of the jaundice
a bird of a yellowish-green colour
commander of a troop of horse,
τὸ τῆς γυναικὸς ἐφήβαιον δηλοῖ, καὶ κόσμον γυναικεῖον παρὰ Κῴοις
Ilium, the city of Ilus, Troy
bind up, make into a bundle
piece of timber used instead of a bond-stone
several straps put round the hand
one who lets out actor's costumes,
collector of deliveries in kind in the form of clothing
drawn out as from a well
the country on the banks of the Indus
κόρην μικράν, καὶ τὴν ἐν τῷ ὀφθαλμῷ
corresponding to, on the same side as
tragedies on the subject of I.
Bacchus invoked with the cry of
root of a hair, young hair
eruption on the face, which often accompanies the first growth of the beard
down, the first growth of the whiskers and beard
φρύγιον, οἱ δὲ τὴν ὑπόκαυσιν τοῦ ἰπνοῦ
oven-maker, fire-clay moulder, worker in terra-cotta
the piece of wood that falls and catches
to serve as a body-guard for the kings
to be broken in for riding
a squadron of horse such as he commands
fit for horses, fit for riding
one who fights from a chariot
one who drives in a chariot-race
driver of horses, one who fights from a chariot
one who fights from a chariot
ass which has been suckled by a mare
played to a mare, while she was being covered
monster with horse's body and fish's tail
travelling by means of horses: driver of horses
to be in ferior in horse
sounding with the tramp of horses
having given birth to a horse
τὰς τριχίνους σειρὰς Πάρθυι οὕτω καλοῦσιν
thorn-apple, Datura stramonium, of which horses are madly fond
small black fleshy substance on the forehead of a new-born foal
mucous humour that runs from mares a-horsing
figure-of-eight curve described by a planet
one who leads a horse by the rein
the art of driving the war-chariot
the white variety of it, I. florentina
the fibrous vessels in the muscles
the same number of times, as many times
equal multiplied by equal multiplied by equal
taking the same time to rise
like a star, bright as a star
in the same number of ways
to be of an intermediate age
enjoying equal right of speech, freedom of speech
big-bellied bottle with a long neck
on the Isthmus: at the Isthmian games,
warden of the temple of Isis,
fungus growing on oaks and walnut-trees
of equal height in relation to the land
dividing equally, giving to all alike
equal to that of a tree
of the same number and person
with the legs on a level
of equal power, possessing equal rights with others
a sentence consisting of equal members
formed of an equal number of strands
like those rendered to the Olympians
like a child, as of a child
of the length of a cubit
having an equal number of sides
reckoned as of equal value with wheat
to be equally balanced, in equipoise
with his legs of the same length
that can be divided into two equal parts, even
that rolls itself up like a bean
have the same number of syllables
having the same number of syllables
coming at the last to all alike
drawing equal weights, equal in strength
level with the surface of the water
have the same number of timeunits
consisting of the same number of time-units
like gold, worth its weight in gold
by an equal number of votes
in which the values of letters added together make up the same sum
bring to a standstill, stay, check
come to a stop, rest satisfied
there is a stop, one comes to a stop
to which the mast was bound
the long beams of the loom
give an account of what one has learnt, record
written account of one's inquiries, narrative, history
web of a certain size, piece,
one who knows law and right, judge
plant used as a styptic, Andropogon ischaemum
fleshy parts round the hip-joint, haunches
the projecting part of the os innominatum
having an impediment in one's speech
ἱστία εἰς ἃ τοὺς ἱστοὺς διατείνουσιν,
decorated with a pattern of willow-leaves
οἷς <ἐν>τείνουσι τὰς ὤας τοῦ ὑφαινομένου μυγελεῖς
one who prophesies by means of fish
follow on the track of, emulate
one who sings with soft Ionic modulations
